description: Phase 4a — PQC Security Hardening: ML-DSA-65 auth tokens, hybrid PQC TLS (X25519MLKEM768 via Caddy), scoped API keys with table-level permissions

userStories.title: Install liboqs-python and ML-DSA-65 key generation
userStories.description: As the system, I need ML-DSA-65 keypair generation so auth tokens can be signed with post-quantum signatures.
acceptanceCriteria: liboqs-python added to backend dependencies via uv add liboqs-python
New function generate_mldsa65_keypair() in services/auth.py returns (private_key, public_key) bytes
Keypair is generated on app startup and stored in app.state
Existing Ed25519 keypair generation preserved for backward compatibility during migration
Unit test: keypair generation produces valid ML-DSA-65 keys (verify signature round-trip)
Unit tests pass
CI passes
Typecheck passes
Production build succeeds
userStories.notes: Merged PR #108. CI green (liboqs built from source). 10 tests (7 unit + 3 startup). Ed25519 preserved.

userStories.title: ML-DSA-65 token signing and verification
userStories.description: As a developer, I want auth tokens signed with ML-DSA-65 so they're quantum-resistant.
acceptanceCriteria: create_access_token and create_refresh_token sign with ML-DSA-65 instead of Ed25519
Token format: custom JWT-like structure (header.payload.signature) since PyJWT doesn't support ML-DSA
Header includes {alg:ML-DSA-65,typ:JWT} base64url encoded
Signature is ML-DSA-65 over header.payload base64url encoded
decode_token verifies ML-DSA-65 signature and validates claims (exp, type, sub)
Tokens are larger (~4.6KB total) — verify this works with HTTP Authorization headers
Developer auth middleware updated to verify ML-DSA-65 tokens
User auth middleware updated to verify ML-DSA-65 tokens
Integration tests: login returns valid ML-DSA-65 token, token accepted by protected endpoints
Unit tests pass
Integration tests pass
CI passes
Typecheck passes
Production build succeeds
Service responds to health check

userStories.title: Dashboard and SDK token compatibility
userStories.description: As a developer using the dashboard or SDK, I want the larger ML-DSA-65 tokens to work seamlessly.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard auth-store handles larger tokens in sessionStorage without size limits
Dashboard api-client sends larger Authorization headers without issues
SDK HttpClient sends larger Authorization headers without issues
MCP server OAuth flow uses POST-based token exchange (ML-DSA-65 tokens are ~4.6KB, too large for URL query params)
Envelope encryption flow still works (PBKDF2 + token refresh unchanged)
Unit tests pass
CI passes
Typecheck passes
Production build succeeds
Verify in browser: login → navigate → auto-decrypt still works
userStories.notes: Merged PR #114. POST-based MCP OAuth, large token tests for sessionStorage/headers/SDK.

userStories.title: Add Caddy reverse proxy with PQC TLS to Docker Compose
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all external traffic encrypted with hybrid PQC TLS so the transport layer is quantum-resistant.
acceptanceCriteria: New infra/Caddyfile with reverse proxy routes: /v1/* → backend:8000, /* → dashboard:3000
Caddy service added to infra/compose.yaml with tls internal for dev certificates
Caddy uses Go 1.24+ which negotiates X25519MLKEM768 by default — no explicit PQC config needed
Volume for Caddy data (certificate persistence across restarts)
Ports: Caddy exposes 443 (HTTPS) and 80 (HTTP redirect)
Backend/dashboard/MCP stay on HTTP internally (Caddy terminates TLS)
WebSocket connections for realtime proxied correctly through Caddy
Health check endpoint accessible through Caddy
Unit tests pass
CI passes
Production build succeeds
userStories.notes: Merged PR #106. All CI green. Review: routing correct, tls internal working, PQC via Go 1.24+ default.

userStories.title: Update dev environment for HTTPS
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the local dev environment to work over HTTPS through Caddy.
acceptanceCriteria: Helper script infra/init-scripts/trust-caddy-ca.sh extracts and installs Caddy dev CA certificate
Dashboard Vite config updated — remove /v1 proxy (Caddy handles routing)
.env.example updated with PQDB_BASE_URL=https://localhost
Backend config.py CORS origins updated to include https://localhost
Backend WebAuthn origin updated to https://localhost
MCP server accepts https:// project URLs
Dashboard MCP callback validation allows https://localhost
NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS documented for Node.js processes to trust Caddy CA
Unit tests pass
CI passes
Typecheck passes
Production build succeeds
Verify in browser: full app works over https://localhost
userStories.notes: Merged PR #109. Caddy uses host.docker.internal for hot-reload. CORS tightened. Passkey CI fix: Playwright env passthrough.

userStories.title: PQC TLS verification
userStories.description: As the system, I need to verify that TLS connections actually negotiate PQC key exchange.
acceptanceCriteria: Test script verifies X25519MLKEM768 key exchange via openssl s_client
Integration test: connect to Caddy endpoint, verify non-404 response over HTTPS
Documentation: which clients negotiate PQC (Chrome 131+, Firefox 132+, Safari 18.4+, Node 23+)
Documentation: Node 22 falls back to X25519 (data is ML-KEM encrypted at app layer regardless)
Unit tests pass
CI passes
Production build succeeds

userStories.title: Scoped API keys — database schema
userStories.description: As the system, I need a permissions schema on API keys so keys can be scoped to specific tables and operations.
acceptanceCriteria: Alembic migration adds permissions JSONB column to api_keys table (nullable, default null = full access)
ApiKey model updated with permissions: Mapped[dict | None]
Permissions format: {tables: {tablename: [select, insert, update, delete]}} or null for full access
Null permissions = backward compatible (existing anon/service keys work unchanged)
Unit test: model accepts permissions dict and defaults to None
Unit tests pass
CI passes
Typecheck passes
Production build succeeds
userStories.notes: Merged PR #107. All CI green. Migration 008, JSONB nullable column, 5 unit tests.

userStories.title: Scoped API key creation and management endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to create API keys with table-level permissions.
acceptanceCriteria: New endpoint POST /v1/projects/{id}/keys/scoped accepts {name, permissions} and returns new key
Permissions schema validated: operations must be select/insert/update/delete
Endpoint GET /v1/projects/{id}/keys returns permissions for each key
Endpoint DELETE /v1/projects/{id}/keys/{key_id} deletes a scoped key
Scoped keys use format pqdb_scoped_{random} to distinguish from anon/service
Integration tests: create scoped key, verify permissions returned in list
Integration tests: scoped key validates against the permissions schema
Unit tests pass
Integration tests pass
CI passes
Typecheck passes
Production build succeeds
Service responds to health check
userStories.notes: Merged PR #110. DELETE guarded to scoped-only, name validation added. 44 tests (22 unit + 22 integration).

userStories.title: Enforce scoped key permissions in middleware
userStories.description: As the system, I want scoped API key permissions enforced so keys can only access the tables/operations they're scoped to.
userStories.passes_note: Merged PR #113. 4 CRUD endpoints enforced. Service bypass, null=full-access backward compat.
acceptanceCriteria: get_project_context middleware updated: parse permissions from matched key
ProjectContext extended with permissions: dict | None field
CRUD endpoints (select, insert, update, delete) check permissions before executing
If key has permissions and table/operation not allowed → 403 with clear error message
If key has null permissions (legacy anon/service) → full access (backward compatible)
Service keys always have full access regardless of permissions field
Integration tests: scoped key can access allowed table, rejected for disallowed table
Integration tests: scoped key with select-only rejected for insert/update/delete
Unit tests pass
Integration tests pass
CI passes
Typecheck passes
Production build succeeds
Service responds to health check
userStories.passes_note: Merged PR #113. 4 CRUD endpoints enforced. Service bypass, null=full-access backward compat.

userStories.title: Dashboard UI for scoped API keys
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to create and manage scoped API keys from the dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: API Keys page shows all keys with their permissions (or Full access for null)
Create Scoped Key dialog: select tables, check operations per table, name the key
Key displayed once on creation (same pattern as existing keys — show once, copy)
Delete button for scoped keys
Existing anon/service keys shown as Full access (not editable)
Unit tests pass
CI passes
Typecheck passes
Production build succeeds
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: Merged PR #115. 9 unit tests, verified in browser via Claude in Chrome.
